title: Functions, Multiple Results, Closures, Recursion, and Contracts · Runtime Contract: Function Signatures Values
summary: A rule engine needs small composable functions, explicit failure results, isolated stateful closures, and bounded recursive traversal. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go function signatures values increment in a composable rule and transformation library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design function signatures, named types, parameters, results, and first-class function values around one responsibility.
Predict deferred-call argument capture, last-in-first-out execution, named-result interaction, and cleanup timing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
